---
title: "Supported Languages"
---

## 14 Mayan Languages of Guatemala

MayaVoice supports translation between Spanish and 14 officially recognized Mayan languages, covering the four major linguistic branches present in Guatemala. Together, these languages are spoken by over 6 million people — roughly 40% of Guatemala's population.

## By Linguistic Family

### K'ichean Branch

The largest branch, including Guatemala's most widely spoken Mayan languages.

::: {.lang-grid}

::: {.lang-card}
[K'iche']{.lang-name}
[1,100,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 21.32]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Q'eqchi']{.lang-name}
[800,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 27.25]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Kaqchikel]{.lang-name}
[450,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 41.34]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Tz'utujil]{.lang-name}
[90,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 2.86]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Achi]{.lang-name}
[150,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 8.30]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Poqomchi']{.lang-name}
[115,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 15.15]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Poqomam]{.lang-name}
[50,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 11.21]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Sipakapense]{.lang-name}
[8,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 15.75]{.lang-bleu}
:::

:::

### Mamean Branch

Languages spoken primarily in Guatemala's western highlands.

::: {.lang-grid}

::: {.lang-card}
[Mam]{.lang-name}
[530,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 47.07]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Awakateko]{.lang-name}
[20,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 15.46]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Tektiteko]{.lang-name}
[5,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 15.76]{.lang-bleu}
:::

:::

### Q'anjob'alan Branch

Languages spoken in the Cuchumatanes highlands of Huehuetenango.

::: {.lang-grid}

::: {.lang-card}
[Q'anjob'al]{.lang-name}
[170,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 23.38]{.lang-bleu}
:::

::: {.lang-card}
[Chuj]{.lang-name}
[65,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 31.32]{.lang-bleu}
:::

:::

### Yucatecan Branch

The only representative of this branch in Guatemala.

::: {.lang-grid}

::: {.lang-card}
[Itza']{.lang-name}
[~1,000 speakers]{.lang-speakers}
[BLEU: 4.02]{.lang-bleu}
:::

:::

## Language Vitality

The languages supported by MayaVoice span the full spectrum of vitality:

- **Thriving** — K'iche' (1.1M), Q'eqchi' (800K), and Mam (530K) are actively spoken across generations and growing.
- **Stable** — Kaqchikel, Q'anjob'al, and Achi maintain strong intergenerational transmission.
- **Vulnerable** — Poqomam, Awakateko, and Tektiteko face pressure from Spanish dominance.
- **Critically endangered** — Itza' has fewer than 1,000 fluent speakers, most elderly. Every digital resource created for Itza' contributes to its preservation.

MayaVoice serves both as a practical translation tool for thriving languages and as a digital preservation resource for endangered ones.
